item.title: How the Premier League became a UK economic asset
item.description: Top-tier English football is a success story and important cultural export

item.title: How AI is forcing McKinsey and its peers to rethink pricing
item.description: Clients are questioning the value of advice while getting more used to fees based on successful task completion

item.title: If you’re in a spiral, you’re doing it right
item.description: What if, despite all your mistakes, you were actually getting somewhere but in a slightly more roundabout way?

item.title: Please stop talking about Gen Z in the office
item.description: Mindless generational labels don’t help at a time when the workplace is one of the few places where young and old mix

item.title: Bond slump stirs vigilante fears
item.description: A sense is setting in among investment professionals that we have a serious problem here

item.title: Swatch and Audemars Piguet’s collab provides a much-needed pop
item.description: The launch may not have gone quite according to plan, but such tie-ups are a good way to inject some buzz into the market

item.title: US crime and mortality is declining fast — what can the rest of us learn?
item.description: America is making progress in driving down violent deaths and those from some dangerous diseases

item.title: Get ready to take sides in Big Tech’s giant IPO bake-off
item.description: With SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic targeting the same market, investors must decide what qualities they prize most

item.title: The new Luddite movement
item.description: If governments don’t slow AI down, voters — like their predecessors — might take matters into their own hands

item.title: Arsenal’s hold on the streets and the elites
item.description: How the team achieved such cultural reach in an unsuccessful era

item.title: What is it that we really hunger for?
item.description: Answering this question can help us make important changes to our lives

item.title: Time to ‘do the splits’ on your mortgage?
item.description: Overstretched borrowers are finding ways to alleviate the pain of higher interest rates

item.title: Air travel sucks — now more than ever
item.description: When it comes to getting on a plane, you cannot lower your expectations enough

item.title: ‘I really should return for the full, big-ticket experience’ — Jay Rayner reviews The Cocochine, London
item.description: Larry Jayasekara’s accomplished cooking and prix fixe lunch offer Mayfair diners excellent return on investment

item.title: New Zealand makes its case for Pinot Noir
item.description: Long associated with Sauvignon Blanc, NZ is producing Burgundian-style reds well worth exploring

item.title: The risks to the legacy of Barney Frank
item.description: A key feature of the annual stress test for US banks is being undermined

item.title: The boutique bank that eschews blockbuster bragging rights
item.description: Lincoln International may not be a household name but it occupies a niche in the market

item.title: To infinity and beyond, with the SpaceX IPO
item.description: Ordinary investors can at last bet directly on AI’s potential — with caveats

item.title: America’s ‘simultaneity’ nightmare
item.description: Trump’s pre-emptive attacks are a traumatic break with the ideals of a globalised world but the playbook is not new

item.title: If you think you understand bonds, you don’t
item.description: They’re too complex for me too — but here are five common traps to avoid

item.title: What is Labour’s ‘soft left’ anyway?
item.description: The UK governing party’s hard-to-define faction is newly powerful

item.title: Why does everyone expect a tip?
item.description: If people are being more brazen about asking, it’s time to respond in kind

item.title: Carson Block: ‘Maybe the dumb money is the smart money’
item.description: The short seller warns of an AI-created financial crisis

item.title: Investing in the era of scarcity
item.description: Markets have yet to register the new fashion among governments for hoarding and huddling

item.title: Beijing’s unlikely bet on Russia may yet pay off
item.description: Putin’s mis-steps have locked it into a relationship with China that is increasingly one-sided
